name: Astolfo
description: Astolfo, a paladin of Charlemagne, is a prominent figure in the Matter of France, particularly in the epic poems of the Carolingian cycle. Renowned for his stunning beauty, vanity, and impulsive nature, Astolfo is often depicted as a somewhat unreliable but ultimately good-hearted hero. He is the son of Otto, King of England, and is known for his incredible skills in horsemanship and his possession of magical artifacts. Astolfo's most significant adventure involves his journey to the moon to retrieve Orlando's lost sanity, a quest he undertakes with the aid of Saint John the Evangelist and the hippogriff. He is often portrayed as a skilled warrior, but his intelligence and judgment are frequently questioned, leading to both humorous and perilous situations. Despite his flaws, Astolfo is a beloved character, embodying a spirit of adventure and highlighting the importance of courage and friendship. He is associated with themes of chivalry, madness, and the power of redemption. His adventures are chronicled most famously in Ludovico Ariosto's Orlando Furioso, where he plays a pivotal role in restoring order to the world after Orlando's descent into madness. He is also associated with magic and the power of divine intervention.
subtitle: The handsome, impulsive paladin of Charlemagne

keyMyths.title: The Journey to the Moon
keyMyths.description: Astolfo, guided by Saint John the Evangelist, travels to the moon on Elijah's chariot to retrieve Orlando's lost wits. He finds them stored in a bottle and brings them back to Earth.

keyMyths.title: Taming the Hippogriff
keyMyths.description: Astolfo is one of the few who manages to tame and ride the Hippogriff, a mythical creature that is half-eagle and half-horse. He uses it for his aerial adventures.

keyMyths.title: The Magic Horn
keyMyths.description: Astolfo possesses a magic horn whose sound can terrify enemies and cause them to flee. He uses it strategically in battles and dangerous situations.

sources.author: Matteo Maria Boiardo
sources.description: Precursor to Orlando Furioso, featuring early versions of Astolfo's character.
sources.text: Precursor to Orlando Furioso, featuring early versions of Astolfo's character.
